2026-04-20T07:22:26.6778807Z 
2026-04-20T07:22:42.8098180Z Now I have enough context. The push introduces meaningful new API behavior that's not reflected in the README:
2026-04-20T07:22:42.8098746Z 
2026-04-20T07:22:42.8101835Z 1. `ResourceNotFoundException` gained new public `Code` and `Param` properties
2026-04-20T07:22:42.8351944Z 2. `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` now fail-fast with 404/400 before handlers run — a behavioral change handler authors must know
2026-04-20T07:22:43.7104094Z 3. The Troubleshooting section's 404 entry only covers response ID TTL expiry, not the new `previous_response_id` validation case
2026-04-20T07:23:16.1653527Z 4. No docs on how provider implementers should populate the new `Code`/`Param` fields on `ResourceNotFoundException`
2026-04-20T07:23:16.1654140Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:17.3909988Z ● create_issue (MCP: safeoutputs) · title: "[AgentServer] Docs: document eager previous_response_id…
2026-04-20T07:23:17.3910948Z   └ {"result":"success"}
2026-04-20T07:23:17.3911120Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:21.2337339Z ● dispatch_triage (MCP: safeoutputs)
2026-04-20T07:23:21.2337779Z   └ {"result":"success"}
2026-04-20T07:23:21.2337955Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:24.7941977Z Filed issue **[AgentServer] Docs: document eager previous_response_id validation and ResourceNotFoundException.Code/Param** for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and dispatched triage.
2026-04-20T07:23:24.7942562Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:25.8736945Z **Gaps identified from PR #58347 (beta.3 release):**
2026-04-20T07:23:25.8741582Z - Troubleshooting section missing the new 404/400 scenarios from eager `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` validation
2026-04-20T07:23:28.3364563Z - `ResponseContext.GetHistoryAsync()` description doesn't mention the new pre-handler caching/validation guarantee
2026-04-20T07:23:28.3368605Z - No docs for the new `ResourceNotFoundException.Code` and `Param` properties that provider implementers need
2026-04-20T07:23:28.3402982Z - No note that non-4xx storage errors are normalized to HTTP 500

2026-04-20T07:23:32.0510031Z   
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0510190Z   Key changes in this commit:
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0510513Z   1. Eagerly validate `previous_response_id` and `conversation_id` before handler invocation
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0510963Z   2. Preserve full Foundry storage error body (code, message, param, type) in client responses
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0511353Z   3. `ResourceNotFoundException` gains `Code` and `Param` properties
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0511632Z   4. New error propagation mechanics

2026-04-20T07:23:32.0517126Z   1. Getting started - ✅ Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server (though it says "Configure the server" not "Authenticate the client")
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0517629Z   2. Key concepts - ✅
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0517834Z   3. Examples - ✅ (brief)
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0518024Z   4. Troubleshooting - ✅
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0518206Z   5. Next steps - ✅
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0518367Z   6. Contributing - ✅
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0518517Z   
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0519165Z   The README is missing "Authenticate the client" in the Getting started section. This is a server library that doesn't authenticate a client per se, but there might be topics around how the server handles authentication/authorization from clients.
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0519864Z   
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0519987Z   
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0521479Z   The bigger issue is that the beta.3 features aren't documented in the README at all—the changelog mentions eager validation of `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id`, plus error preservation, but the Troubleshooting section doesn't explain how the 404 for invalid `previous_response_id` works or how error fields get preserved. The new `ResourceNotFoundException.Code` and `ResourceNotFoundException.Param` properties also aren't mentioned anywhere in the docs.
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0522691Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0523039Z ✓ github-get_commit(include_diff: true, owner: Azure, repo: azure-sdk-for-net, sha: 16a9c8b7e752cd0375e7770aa2b0447d3b7193b0)
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0523373Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0523575Z ◆ Now I understand the full picture. The push to main consists of two commits:
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0523898Z   1. `16a9c8b` - The actual code changes (PR #58341)
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0524219Z   2. `d36e0c2` - The release prep commit (PR #58347) - just changelog wording fix
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0524487Z   
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0524941Z   The triggering push is PR #58347 which includes all the code from PR #58341.
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0525207Z   
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0525336Z   Key code changes:
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0525609Z   - `ResourceNotFoundException` now has `Code` and `Param` properties (new public API)
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0525998Z   - `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` are now eagerly validated
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0526446Z   - Storage error fields preserved (code, message, param, type)
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0526691Z   
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0526812Z   
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0527364Z   - The README doesn't document these new exception properties or explain that `previous_response_id` validation now happens before the handler runs, which is a behavioral change from what's currently documented.
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0528677Z   - The Troubleshooting section conflates two different 404 scenarios—response expiration and invalid `previous_response_id`—without distinguishing between them.
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0529934Z   - I need to check if there's a provider implementation guide that covers `ResourceNotFoundException` and whether it needs updates for the new `Code` and `Param` properties.
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0531266Z   - The eager validation of `previous_response_id` against `conversation.id` is a behavioral change that should be documented alongside the other 404 cases.
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0531903Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0532410Z ◆ Now I have enough context. The push introduces meaningful new API behavior that's not reflected in the README:
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0533054Z   
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0533476Z   1. `ResourceNotFoundException` gained new public `Code` and `Param` properties
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0534720Z   2. `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` now fail-fast with 404/400 before handlers run — a behavioral change handler authors must know
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0535807Z   3. The Troubleshooting section's 404 entry only covers response ID TTL expiry, not the new `previous_response_id` validation case
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0536483Z   4. No docs on how provider implementers should populate the new `Code`/`Param` fields on `ResourceNotFoundException`
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0536876Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0537239Z ✓ safeoutputs-create_issue(title: [AgentServer] Docs: document eager previ..., body: ## Documentation Gap
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0537559Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0537626Z **Package:** `Azur...)
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0537752Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0537864Z ✓ safeoutputs-dispatch_triage
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0538013Z 
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0538583Z ◆ Filed issue **[AgentServer] Docs: document eager previous_response_id validation and ResourceNotFoundException.Code/Param** for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and dispatched triage.
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0539180Z   
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0539366Z   **Gaps identified from PR #58347 (beta.3 release):**
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0539840Z   - Troubleshooting section missing the new 404/400 scenarios from eager `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` validation
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0540471Z   - `ResponseContext.GetHistoryAsync()` description doesn't mention the new pre-handler caching/validation guarantee
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0541088Z   - No docs for the new `ResourceNotFoundException.Code` and `Param` properties that provider implementers need
2026-04-20T07:23:32.0541553Z   - No note that non-4xx storage errors are normalized to HTTP 500

Made MockResponse.Content non-nullable — uses BinaryData.FromString(body)\\n   even for empty bodies to match real Azure.Core.Response behavior.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Address review comments: fix doc accuracy for conversation.id and error types\\n\\n- Fix test doc: 'conversation_id' → 'conversation.id' to match protocol field name\\n- Expand ValidatingProvider summary to describe both 404 and 400 paths\\n- Update handler comment to reflect multiple possible error types (not just 404)\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Fix changelog wording: include 409 in excluded status codes\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n---------\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\",\"author\":{\"name\":\"Ravi Pidaparthi\",\"email\":\"rapida@microsoft.com\",\"date\":\"2026-04-20T07:18:45Z\"},\"committer\":{\"name\":\"GitHub\",\"email\":\"noreply@github.com\",\"date\":\"2026-04-20T07:18:45Z\"}},\"author\":{\"login\":\"RaviPidaparthi\",\"id\":28370364,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/RaviPidaparthi\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/28370364?v=4\"},\"committer\":{\"login\":\"web-flow\",\"id\":19864447,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/web-flow\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4\"},\"stats\":{\"additions\":2,\"deletions\":6,\"total\":8},\"files\":[{\"filename\":\"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/CHANGELOG.md\",\"status\":\"modified\",\"additions\":2,\"deletions\":6,\"changes\":8}]}","type":"text"}]}}}

Add the NuGet package, extend one abstract class (`ResponseHandler`), and the library handles routing, streaming (SSE), background execution, cancellation, caching, and response lifecycle management.\n\n[Source code][source] | [Package (NuGet)][nuget] | [REST API reference][rest_api] | [Product documentation][product_doc]\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\n\nInstall the library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/):\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses --prerelease\n```\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n- An [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/)\n- [.NET 8.0](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) or later\n- An ASP.NET Core application\n\n### Configure the server\n\nThe recommended way to start a Responses server is with the built-in one-line API:\n\n```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConfigureServer_Tier1\nResponsesServer.Run\u003cEchoHandler\u003e();\n```\n\nThis starts a Kestrel server with OpenTelemetry, health checks, server version header, inbound request logging, and your handler mapped to the Responses API endpoints. The `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` package is included as a transitive dependency.\n\nAlternatively, use `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` for more control over service registration and middleware:\n\n```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConfigureServer_Manual\nvar builder = AgentHost.CreateBuilder();\nbuilder.AddResponses\u003cEchoHandler\u003e();\nbuilder.Build().Run();\n```\n\n## Key concepts\n\n### ResponseHand

This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully."
2026-04-20T07:23:32.2507466Z       },
